refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation in the problem domain; recording and managing are implementation concerns that belong in the DP/IM layers. The refined FR focuses on the core functional need: translating worksite mission inputs into explicit performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability). This remains solution-neutral and testable against worksite inputs.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that given a worksite mission context (e.g., site layout, production targets, safety constraints), the system produces a documented set of performance targets that are traceable to those inputs and satisfy acceptance criteria (documented, traceable, baselined).
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall accept worksite mission context inputs (e.g., production targets, site constraints, safety policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall establish traceability links between derived performance requirements and source worksite inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall maintain a baseline record of performance requirements and calibration updates.
refined_fr.note: The refined FR is atomic at the derivation level. Sub-FRs 005.1–005.4 decompose the full obligation chain (input → compute → trace → record) to enable independent DP/IM assignment and verification. Sub-FR 005.4 (recording/history) is a supporting functional requirement that ensures traceability and auditability, which are part of the problem domain given the acceptance criteria.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a mission-context capture module that accepts worksite inputs (safety plan, operational policies, environmental conditions) and applies a deterministic mapping function to derive performance targets; maintain a versioned baseline registry with change history and traceability links.

DP-B: Establish a parameterized performance-target template library indexed by worksite class/mission type; populate templates via structured intake questionnaire; store all instantiations with audit trail and calibration metadata.

DP-C: Deploy a rule-based inference engine that ingests worksite operational policies and safety constraints as declarative rules, executes logical derivation of performance thresholds, and logs all rule firings and target decisions with full traceability.

DP-D: Create a federated data model where worksite mission context is stored in a canonical repository; performance targets are computed on-demand via a stateless derivation service; all versions and updates are immutably recorded in a time-series audit log.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Create a federated data model where worksite mission context is stored in a canonical repository; performance targets are computed on-demand via a stateless derivation service; all versions and updates are immutably recorded in a time-series audit log.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between mission-context schema and derivation logic if rules are hard-coded rather than externalized.
Dependency on audit-log persistence layer; failure modes must be defined (e.g., log unavailability does not block target computation).
Interface contract between canonical repository and derivation service must be versioned to allow independent evolution.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D achieves low coupling by separating concerns: (1) mission-context storage is independent of derivation logic, (2) stateless derivation service allows horizontal scaling and testability, (3) immutable audit log decouples compliance/traceability from operational performance. Risks are mitigated by explicit interface versioning and fallback strategies for log unavailability.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context inputs are well-formed and validated before ingestion into canonical repository; no assumption of real-time streaming updates.
ASSUMPTION: Performance-target derivation logic is deterministic and does not require human judgment or iterative refinement during normal operation.

RISK: If worksite safety plan or operational policies change frequently, the canonical repository schema may require frequent migration; mitigation: use schema-versioning and backward-compatibility patterns.
RISK: Audit-log growth may become a storage/query bottleneck; mitigation: implement log retention policies and archival strategy aligned with regulatory requirements.
RISK: Traceability from worksite inputs to derived targets may be lost if derivation rules are not explicitly documented; mitigation: require rule versioning and change-control process.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR-NGHE-005 to explicitly state: (1) the expected frequency and scope of worksite context changes (e.g., 'mission context is updated quarterly or upon safety-plan revision'), (2) the required retention period for baseline history and audit logs (e.g., '5 years or per regulatory mandate'), and (3) the acceptable latency for performance-target derivation (e.g., 'on-demand within 5 minutes' vs. 'pre-computed daily'). This will inform IM-layer decisions (e.g., batch vs. streaming, caching strategy, log archival) and reduce ambiguity in acceptance criteria.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Federated data model repository, stateless performance derivation service, and immutable time-series audit log for worksite mission context, performance targets, and all state changes across the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingest worksite telemetry and mission context updates into the canonical repository; feed performance derivation service with real-time sensor data.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive task definitions and mission parameters; return computed performance targets and derived mission constraints to tasking subsystem.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Emit audit log entries and performance threshold violations to safety services for reactive decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Synchronize federated repository state across distributed nodes; replicate audit log entries for consistency and recovery.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level orchestration layer responsible for mission planning, data integration, and cross-subsystem coordination. The federated data model, canonical repository, and audit log are natural system-wide concerns that belong at this level. Stateless derivation service fits cleanly as a utility within NGHE. Minor diffusion: Telemetry ingestion and Tasking integration require external interface contracts, but logic remains contained.

Vehicle module is responsible for platform-specific actuation, propulsion, and local state. A federated mission context repository and system-wide audit log are not vehicle concerns. Placing DP here would require Vehicle to expose mission-level data and versioning logic, violating separation of concerns. High diffusion: performance targets and audit records would need to escape to NGHE and Safety Services.

Teleoperation module handles remote operator interface and command relay. It is not responsible for canonical data storage, performance derivation, or audit logging. Placing DP here would scatter mission context logic across operator-facing concerns. High diffusion: audit log and performance targets must propagate to NGHE and Safety Services.

Safety Services consumes performance targets and audit events to enforce safety constraints. While it is a natural consumer of the audit log and derived performance data, it should not own the canonical repository or derivation logic. Placing DP here inverts the dependency: Safety Services would become a data authority rather than a policy enforcer. Moderate-high diffusion: mission context and performance derivation logic would need to be replicated or exposed externally.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The federated data model, canonical repository, stateless performance derivation service, and immutable audit log are system-level concerns that naturally belong in the NGHE Solution module. NGHE is the integration point for mission planning, worksite context, and cross-subsystem coordination. The DP is cohesive within NGHE because all three elements (repository, derivation, audit) serve the same purpose: maintaining a single source of truth for mission state and enabling deterministic, auditable performance reasoning. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined contract boundaries; no core logic diffuses outside NGHE.
containment_risks: If the canonical repository grows to include vehicle-specific state (e.g., propulsion parameters, sensor calibration), Vehicle module may need read access or even co-ownership of repository partitions. Mitigation: enforce strict schema boundaries and use federated views to isolate vehicle-local data.

If Safety Services requires real-time audit log writes (e.g., to record safety decisions), the audit log may become a shared resource with bidirectional coupling. Mitigation: Safety Services writes to a separate safety-specific log; NGHE audit log remains unidirectional (system state only).

If Teleoperation operators need to query historical performance targets or audit trails for debugging, Teleoperation may develop tight coupling to the repository. Mitigation: expose read-only query interfaces via Tasking or a dedicated audit query service.

Stateless derivation service assumes all inputs are available in the canonical repository. If real-time performance computation requires low-latency local caching or edge computation, the service may need to diffuse into Vehicle or Teleoperation. Mitigation: design derivation service with pluggable data sources and latency SLAs.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: High. The IM boundary (NGHE Solution) is explicitly scoped to include federated data model, stateless derivation service, and immutable audit log. Acceptance criteria require documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained history of baselines/calibration updates. These are directly verifiable through: (1) audit log inspection for traceability chains from worksite mission context to derived targets, (2) schema validation of canonical repository for immutability guarantees, (3) derivation service output logs for deterministic computation records. The time-series audit log provides the primary verification artifact.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear. FR-CR-NGHE-005 maps unambiguously to NGHE Solution as the owning module. The three DP components (repository, derivation service, audit log) are co-located in a single architectural boundary with well-defined external interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking in/out, SafetyAlert out, Network Link in/out). No ambiguity exists regarding which module is responsible for performance derivation from mission context.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D directly addresses FR-CR-NGHE-005 with sufficient logical completeness. The three-part design (canonical repository + stateless derivation + immutable audit) covers the full requirement scope: (1) 'derive performance requirements' is satisfied by the stateless derivation service, (2) 'from worksite mission context' is satisfied by the canonical repository storing mission context, (3) traceability and history are satisfied by the immutable time-series audit log. The DP is solution-neutral (does not prescribe specific technologies) and is testable against acceptance criteria (traceable targets, maintained history).

diffusion_risks: Vehicle module containment risk (diffusion_score 0.85): If canonical repository schema expands to include vehicle-specific state (propulsion parameters, sensor calibration), Vehicle may require co-ownership or write access to repository partitions. This would violate the single-authority principle. Mitigation: enforce strict schema boundaries via federated views; Vehicle reads only its own partition; NGHE owns mission-level schema.

Safety Services bidirectional coupling risk (diffusion_score 0.65): If Safety Services requires real-time writes to the audit log (e.g., to record safety decisions or constraint violations), the audit log becomes a shared resource with bidirectional coupling. This inverts the dependency: Safety Services would become a data authority rather than a consumer. Mitigation: Safety Services writes to a separate safety-specific event log; NGHE audit log remains unidirectional (system state and performance derivation only).

Teleoperation query coupling risk (diffusion_score 0.8): If remote operators need to query historical performance targets or audit trails for debugging/mission review, Teleoperation may develop tight coupling to the canonical repository. This creates an unintended consumer dependency. Mitigation: expose read-only query interfaces via a dedicated audit query service or through Tasking interface; do not expose repository directly to Teleoperation.

Stateless derivation service latency risk: The DP assumes all inputs are available in the canonical repository and derivation is on-demand. If real-time performance computation requires sub-100ms latency or edge computation, the service may need to diffuse into Vehicle or Teleoperation for local caching. Mitigation: design derivation service with pluggable data sources and explicit latency SLAs; if edge computation is required, create a separate edge derivation module with clear data sync contracts to NGHE.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution exhibits strong containment (score 0.92, diffusion 0.08). The federated data model, canonical repository, and audit log are cohesive system-level concerns that naturally belong at the integration layer. The stateless derivation service is a utility function that depends on the repository and feeds external consumers (Tasking, SafetyAlert). All four external interfaces are well-defined contract boundaries with clear direction and purpose. No core DP logic diffuses outside NGHE in the nominal design. However, four containment risks are identified above that could cause diffusion if not actively mitigated through schema governance, interface design, and dependency inversion. The recommended mitigations preserve the single-authority principle and prevent bidirectional coupling.

recommended_revisions: Revise DP-D to explicitly state: 'The canonical repository is the single source of truth for worksite mission context and derived performance targets. All other modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) consume repository state via read-only interfaces or event subscriptions. No module other than NGHE writes to the canonical repository or audit log.' This clarifies ownership and prevents diffusion.

Add a sub-DP for audit log partitioning: 'Immutable audit log is partitioned into two streams: (1) NGHE system state and performance derivation events (owned by NGHE), (2) Safety Services constraint enforcement events (owned by Safety Services). Cross-stream queries are permitted; writes are unidirectional per stream.' This prevents bidirectional coupling with Safety Services.

Add a sub-DP for repository schema governance: 'Canonical repository schema is versioned and governed by NGHE. Vehicle-specific state (propulsion, sensor calibration) is stored in a separate Vehicle-owned partition with federated view access from NGHE. Mission context and performance targets remain in NGHE-owned schema.' This prevents Vehicle from claiming co-ownership.

Add a sub-DP for query interface design: 'Read-only query access to canonical repository and audit log is exposed via a dedicated Query Service (owned by NGHE) with explicit latency SLAs and access control policies. Teleoperation and Safety Services query through this service, not directly. This prevents tight coupling and enables future caching/optimization without diffusing DP logic.'

Add acceptance criterion: 'All writes to canonical repository and audit log originate from NGHE Solution module. No other module writes to these resources. Verify via code review and runtime access control logs.' This operationalizes the single-authority principle.

Add acceptance criterion: 'Performance targets are derived deterministically from worksite mission context inputs. Derivation service is stateless and idempotent. Verify by replaying historical mission context through derivation service and confirming output matches audit log records.' This ensures traceability and auditability.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence, and containment is strong (0.92). However, the recommended_revisions identify four material diffusion risks (scores 0.65–0.85) that must be resolved at the current level before decomposition proceeds. These risks are not minor; they directly threaten the single-authority principle and could cause architectural coupling if not explicitly addressed in the DP and acceptance criteria. Safety criticality (high) amplifies the need for clarity on audit log ownership and Safety Services coupling. Revising the current level to incorporate explicit governance statements, partitioning rules, and query interface design will eliminate ambiguity and prevent downstream rework. Once revisions are incorporated and re-gated, decomposition to sub-FRs can proceed with confidence.

revise.why: DP-D (canonical repository + stateless derivation + immutable audit log) is logically sound but lacks explicit governance rules that prevent diffusion. The four recommended revisions must be incorporated as sub-DPs or clarifying statements: (1) single-authority principle for repository writes, (2) audit log partitioning to prevent bidirectional coupling with Safety Services, (3) schema governance to prevent Vehicle co-ownership, (4) query interface design to prevent Teleoperation tight coupling. These revisions do not change the core DP logic but make ownership, data flow, and interface contracts explicit and enforceable. Additionally, two new acceptance criteria must be added to operationalize the single-authority principle and ensure deterministic derivation traceability.

assumptions: NGHE Solution is the single authority for worksite mission context and derived performance targets; no other module writes to the canonical repository or audit log.
Audit log is partitioned into NGHE-owned (system state, performance derivation) and Safety Services-owned (constraint enforcement) streams; writes are unidirectional per stream.

Vehicle-specific state (propulsion, sensor calibration) is stored in a separate Vehicle-owned partition with federated view access from NGHE; Vehicle does not write to mission context or performance target schema.

Read-only query access to canonical repository and audit log is exposed via a dedicated Query Service (NGHE-owned) with explicit latency SLAs; Teleoperation and Safety Services do not query repository directly.
Derivation service is stateless and idempotent; performance targets are derived deterministically from worksite mission context inputs.
All external interfaces (Telemetry in, Tasking in/out, SafetyAlert out, Network Link in/out) are read-only or event-subscription based for consumers; no bidirectional coupling.
open_risks: Vehicle module containment risk (diffusion_score 0.85): If schema governance is not enforced via code review and runtime access control, Vehicle may claim co-ownership of repository partitions. Mitigation: implement federated view enforcement and document schema ownership in architecture decision records.

Safety Services bidirectional coupling risk (diffusion_score 0.65): If Safety Services writes to the main audit log, the log becomes a shared resource and NGHE loses single authority. Mitigation: partition audit log into separate streams and enforce write access control.

Teleoperation query coupling risk (diffusion_score 0.8): If Teleoperation queries the canonical repository directly, tight coupling may develop and future optimization (caching, edge computation) becomes difficult. Mitigation: expose read-only query interfaces via a dedicated Query Service with explicit SLAs.

Stateless derivation service latency risk: If real-time performance computation requires sub-100ms latency, the service may need to diffuse into Vehicle or Teleoperation for local caching. Mitigation: design derivation service with pluggable data sources and explicit latency SLAs; if edge computation is required, create a separate edge derivation module with clear data sync contracts.
evidence_needed: Architecture decision record (ADR) documenting single-authority principle for canonical repository and audit log; signed by NGHE, Vehicle, Safety Services, and Teleoperation stakeholders.

Schema governance policy document specifying: (1) NGHE-owned schema (mission context, performance targets), (2) Vehicle-owned schema (propulsion, sensor calibration), (3) federated view definitions, (4) write access control rules.

Audit log partitioning design document specifying: (1) NGHE system state and performance derivation events, (2) Safety Services constraint enforcement events, (3) cross-stream query rules, (4) write access control per stream.

Query Service interface specification (OpenAPI or equivalent) documenting: (1) read-only endpoints for canonical repository and audit log, (2) latency SLAs, (3) access control policies, (4) rate limiting rules.

Derivation service design document specifying: (1) stateless and idempotent computation, (2) input data sources (canonical repository), (3) output format and traceability to audit log, (4) latency SLAs and edge computation fallback strategy.
Code review checklist for repository write access: all writes must originate from NGHE Solution module; no other module writes to canonical repository or audit log.

Runtime access control logs demonstrating: (1) all writes to canonical repository and audit log originate from NGHE, (2) Vehicle reads only its own partition, (3) Teleoperation and Safety Services query via Query Service, (4) no direct repository access from external modules.
Deterministic derivation verification test: replay historical mission context through derivation service and confirm output matches audit log records; document test results and any discrepancies.
